mnemonics: PETE Preprocesses Every Text Efficiently (Tokenization, Cleaning, Normalization, Feature Extraction)
BOWL of Words (Bag-of-Words model represents text as a word frequency vector)
TFIDF: Term Frequency Inverse Document Frequency (highlights important, unique words)
keyPoints: Text must be converted from unstructured strings to a structured numerical format (vectorization) before a model can use it.
The Bag-of-Words model is a simple but powerful representation that ignores word order and grammar, focusing only on word frequency.
TF-IDF weighting is used to reflect how important a word is to a document in a collection, diminishing the weight of common words.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to perform thorough text cleaning (lowercasing, removing stop words/punctuation) which leads to noisy, inefficient models.
Assuming the Bag-of-Words model understands word context or meaning, which it does not; it's only a statistical representation.
Applying TF-IDF without first creating a simple frequency-based Bag-of-Words model to understand the foundational concept.
